element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses team roster and strategy before gameplay
Core offensive mechanic requiring timing and pitcher prediction
Core defensive mechanic requiring pitch selection and placement
Decision-making for advancing runners and stealing bases
Different teams present varied competitive challenge levels

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Turn-based rhythm with discrete tension peaks during at-bats; relief comes from successful outcomes or inning transitions; nine-inning structure provides natural pacing arcs with potential late-game tension escalation

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible with basic team selection; early innings establish mechanic comprehension
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Difficulty scales based on opponent team strength; pitcher AI improves in response to player patterns
difficulty_curve.late_game: Increased competitive pressure in final innings; closer selection and high-leverage situations create tension
notable_spikes: Transition from known pitchers to relief pitchers with different arsenals
Late-inning situations with runners in scoring position
Championship or final matchup games against stronger opponent teams

notable_level_patterns.description: Standard baseball game divided into nine distinct innings; each inning contains two half-innings (offense/defense) creating recurring tension-release cycles
Different selectable teams present variable skill levels and pitcher arsenals; allows players to self-select challenge progression
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Team selection screen determines all subsequent gameplay difficulty

notable_level_patterns.description: Starting pitcher plays multiple innings; relief pitchers introduced mid-game with distinct throw patterns and speed variations
Game tension directly correlates with score differential; close games maintain sustained pressure throughout; blowouts reduce late-inning tension
